authoritative_sources.title: Administrative Work in the Equipment, Facilities, and Services Group, GS-1600: Cemetery Administration Services, GS-1630
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OPM is the federal classification authority and explicitly defines Cemetery Administration Specialist work, including interments, cemetery operations, essential records, and required recordkeeping systems.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: role boundary, cemetery administration workflow, records-system expectations
used_for: workflow stages for interments, administration, maintenance coordination, and public relations

authoritative_sources.title: New York Not-for-Profit Corporation Law section 1510
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York State Senate Open Legislation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New York statute directly governs cemetery corporation burial records, cremation and natural organic reduction records, monument authorization records, and cemetery disclosure obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: burial record maintenance, disclosure handling, monument and pet-cremated-remain boundaries
used_for: cemetery disclosure and consumer-rights scenario design

authoritative_sources.title: New York Public Health Law section 4145, Deaths; burial and removal permits; disposition of remains
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York State Senate Open Legislation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The statute defines final-disposition permit, body receipt, permit endorsement, return, retention, and inspection-ready record requirements for places of burial or disposition.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: permit validation, body receipt, final-disposition record maintenance, official inspection readiness
used_for: refusal when permit or receipt evidence is missing

authoritative_sources.title: Division of Cemeteries
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York State Department of State
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Division of Cemeteries is the regulator for New York not-for-profit cemeteries and publishes operator resources, forms, annual reporting, disclosure, complaint, and cemetery-board materials.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: operator context, disclosure and reporting, regulatory routing
used_for: tenant facts for regulated cemetery type
disclosure, complaint, and annual reporting boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Cemetery Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York State Department of State
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The regulator explains jurisdiction scope, burial refusal conditions, burial-right inheritance recording, trust monies, service charges, grave liner conditions, and local-rule dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: jurisdiction screening, refusal boundaries, lot-right and fee escalation
used_for: burial denial and service-charge boundary scenarios
recording inherited burial rights without deciding heirship

authoritative_sources.title: Local Government Records Schedule: Cemetery
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The state archives schedule defines retention for cemetery records such as lot-owner records, bills of sale, headstone inscriptions, interment, exhumation, removal records, maps, plot plans, surveys, and monument-work permits.
used_for: permanent-record expectations for lot owners, inscriptions, interments, removals, maps, and surveys

authoritative_sources.title: Schedule a burial for a Veteran or family member
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: VA National Cemetery Administration guidance defines burial scheduling workflow, required evidence, next-of-kin data, burial details, memorial preferences, and when cemetery-specific contact is required.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: burial scheduling, eligibility evidence gap, family and funeral-director coordination

authoritative_sources.title: 38 CFR 38.630, Burial headstones and markers; medallions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal regulation governs Government-furnished burial headstones, markers, medallions, replacement requests, next-of-kin record references, and VA electronic ordering or form pathways.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: memorial marker and inscription evidence handling
used_for: refusal to alter marker or inscription records without authority

authoritative_sources.title: Importation of Human Remains into the U.S. for Burial, Entombment, or Cremation
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the federal public-health authority for human-remains importation and defines document, permit, container, and infectious-disease conditions before remains are consigned to a cemetery or crematory for final resting.
used_for: safety-sensitive handoff to licensed and public-health owners

authoritative_sources.title: Funeral Service Workers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS provides official public occupational workflow terminology for funeral service administration, legal-document filing, burial scheduling, cemetery coordination, and state licensing boundaries.
used_for: adjacent funeral director and cemetery representative boundaries

workflow_stages.name: Tenant and authority readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, cemetery type, regulator scope, facility rules, retention schedule, access policy, authority matrix, update permissions, and systems of record before accepting cemetery administration work.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cemetery record and evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Collect burial or transit permit, death-record reference, body receipt, delivery receipt, interment authorization, lot-right evidence, plot or GIS location, work order, schedule, and family communication records.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completeness and classification review
workflow_stages.description: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked; determine whether records are complete, current, properly classified, and ready for the requested administrative use.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Permitted administrative maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Maintain status fields, missing-item lists, owner assignments, due dates, source links, and audit notes when tenant permissions and source evidence support the change.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict, retention, access, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate conflicting permit, body receipt, lot, map, GIS, ledger, memorial, access, retention, public-health, or authority questions rather than resolving them informally.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Handoff and completion evidence
workflow_stages.description: Prepare cemetery-specific status summaries, evidence packets, record update notes, retention/access packages, and adjacent-owner handoffs with source-record traceability.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

family communication, next-of-kin, authorized-agent, consent, access, complaint, and disclosure records
record update note, evidence packet, audit trail, retention log, access review, and governance escalation
systems_of_record: plot map, plat, survey, GIS, and GPS location system
burial scheduling and public committal schedule system
electronic death registration or vital-record reference system

monument, marker, inscription, and memorial-production system
payment, contract, service-charge, and care-fund reference system
audit log and compliance review system

allowed: classify cemetery administration requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked
request minimum jurisdiction, cemetery-type, source-record, retention, access, and authority facts
determine whether records are complete, current, properly classified, and ready for administrative use
prepare record update notes, evidence packets, missing-item lists, owner assignments, due dates, and workflow summaries
maintain permitted administrative statuses and metadata when source evidence, tenant policy, and update permissions are present
escalate: jurisdiction, cemetery type, religious, municipal, federal, tribal, private, or nonprofit rules materially change the answer
permit, body receipt, lot-right, map, GIS, ledger, work-order, memorial, or family authorization records conflict
retention, privacy, access, disclosure, public-record, or official-inspection rules conflict with the request
legal right of disposition, heirship, lot ownership, burial denial, disinterment, relocation, veteran eligibility, public-health, financial, safety, or licensed authority determines the outcome
refuse: request asks the lane to approve burial, disinterment, relocation, memorial alteration, eligibility, benefits, legal right, financial waiver, or policy override
request asks to alter official cemetery records without required source documents and approval trail
request asks to release restricted cemetery, death, permit, next-of-kin, or family communication records without confirmed authority
request asks to mark a record complete or ready when required permits, receipt, authorization, location, retention, or memorial evidence is missing

domain_failure_modes: marking interment ready without burial or transit permit and body receipt evidence
altering grave, crypt, niche, or lot location based on family recollection when ledger, map, GIS, or deed records conflict
treating cemetery lot ownership or right-of-interment inheritance as an administrative data-cleanup task instead of an authority-sensitive legal issue
releasing recent burial files, next-of-kin data, death certificate images, permits, or family communications without access authorization
destroying or downgrading retention for permanent lot-owner, interment, exhumation, removal, map, survey, or inscription records
approving veteran eligibility, military honors, marker entitlement, burial benefits, or national cemetery placement from incomplete evidence
changing marker, inscription, or monument records without authorized requester, cemetery approval, VA or regulator pathway, and work-order evidence
accepting imported or potentially infectious human remains without required public-health, transit, container, and licensed-owner documentation
turning cemetery administration into generic file sorting and losing permit, body receipt, lot-right, plot-location, and memorialization controls
concealing unresolved payment, fee, service-charge, disclosure, or care-fund blockers behind a completed administrative status

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: State, local, tribal, federal, religious, municipal, private, and nonprofit cemetery rules differ. The pack requires tenant jurisdiction and cemetery type before applying or adapting any public baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: right to control disposition, heirship, and lot ownership
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Authority to control disposition, inherit burial rights, authorize disinterment, or resolve lot disputes is jurisdiction and document specific. The lane records evidence and escalates; it does not decide these issues.

Retention and disclosure obligations vary by cemetery type, public/private status, tenant policy, and record class. The lane refuses or escalates access, release, destruction, or retention changes when authority is not confirmed.

Tenant cemetery management systems, EDR references, GIS, work-order tools, memorial ordering systems, and audit logs define permitted update fields and approvals. The lane cannot act without those runtime permissions.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because variable jurisdiction, cemetery type, authority, retention, public-health, and system rules are converted into tenant-required facts, refusal triggers, and orchestrator-return conditions.
The research source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, retention, public-health, memorialization, jurisdiction, and failure handling rather than relying on the industry label alone.
